text:
## Quality audit result

**Count: 1 issue**

**Issue: ENG-142** — "Primary DB failover is slow" (state: done, assignee: niaj)

**Why it fails the audit:**
- It IS in `done` state
- It IS claimed by pull requests via `closes_linear` (PR #1007 in `acme/infra` and PR #1036 in `acme/web`)
- Neither of those PRs is merged — both are `open`

The other 4 done issues (ENG-122, ENG-125, ENG-131, ENG-134) don't appear in any PR's `closes_linear` field at all, so they aren't "claimed to be closed" by any PR. Only ENG-142 is claimed-closed-by-a-PR-but-not-merged while still marked done.
I'll start by fetching done Linear issues and PRs (merged and open) to see which issues are claimed to be closed by which PRs.

(->> merged-prs first)
"}

text: Good — `closes_linear` is a single string field on each PR. Now let me build the set of issue ids closed by merged PRs, and find done issues that are NOT in it but ARE in some PR's `closes_linear`.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def merged-closes (->> merged-prs (map :closes_linear) (remove nil?) set))
merged-closes
(->> all-prs (map :closes_linear) (remove nil?) distinct)
"}

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def claimed-closes (->> all-prs (map :closes_linear) (remove nil?) distinct set))
claimed-closes
(->> done-issues
     (filter (fn [iss] (and (contains? claimed-closes (:id iss))
                            (not (contains? merged-closes (:id iss))))))
     (map :id))
"}

text: Let me double-check by looking at ENG-142 and the PRs that claim to close it:
Hmm — empty result. Let me check what's claiming ENG-142.
Confirmed — ENG-142 is in `done` state, is claimed by 2 PRs (PR #1007 and #1036), but neither is merged. Let me also verify no other done issue falls into the bucket:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":"
(def final (filter (fn [iss] (and (contains? claimed-closes (:id iss))
                                 (not (contains? merged-closes (:id iss)))))
                   done-issues))
(count final)
(map :id final)
"}
